title: Cytochemical staining of endocytosed HRP, flat embedding
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4213a","text":"In this protocol, neurons are pulse-chased with horseradish peroxidase (HRP) to induce activity-dependent uptake of HRP into endocytic compartments such as synaptic vesicles and endosomes. HRP is visualised via diaminobenzidine cytochemistry followed by osmium tetroxide, producing a dark brown, electron-dense precipitate within labeled structures. After staining, neurons are flat-embedded in LX112 directly on the plastic culture dish.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Chase the HRP uptake in neurons for 10 or 30 minutes in low K⁺ buffer in a cell incubator (37°C with 5% CO₂).

Fix cells with 2% Paraformaldehyde (Electron Microscopy Sciences, 15710), 1.5% Glutaraldehyde (Electron Microscopy Sciences, 16210) in 0.1 M Sodium cacodylate (NaCac, Sigma-Aldrich, C0250) buffer pH 7.4 for 20 minutes at room temperature. NOTE! Use EM-grade, fresh, fixatives only and perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood.
Wash 2 times with 0.1 M NaCac buffer, á 3 min. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood.
Wash 3 times with 50 mM Tris buffer, pH 7.6, á 5 min. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood.

Incubate in DAB solution (with peroxide) for 30 minutes, room temperature, covered from light - Dilute 10 mg DAB tablet (Sigma-Aldrich, D4418) into 10 ml of 50 mM Tris-buffer, pH 7.6 - Just before use add 7 µl of 30% H₂O₂ to 10 ml of diluted DAB-solution to final concentration of 0.021%. NOTE: keep DAB solutions covered from light all the time!
Wash 3 times with Tris buffer, á 5 min.
Wash 2 times with 0.1 M NaCac buffer. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood.

Osmicate using Reduced 1% osmium Tetroxide (OsO₄), 1.5% potassium ferrocyanide (K 4 [Fe(CN) 6 ]) diluted in Millli-q water. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood. Biowave settings: MW 80 W, 2 min ON – 2 min OFF – 2 min ON, VAC on
Repeat step 10
Water wash: Biowave settings: MW 250 W, 40 s No VAC. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood.
Contrast with 2% uranyl acetate (UA) (aq): Biowave settings: MW 150 W, 1 min ON – 1 min OFF – 1 min ON, VAC on (optional). Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood.
Dehydration in a fume hood (no Biowave usage): - 50% EtOH 1min - 60% EtOH 1min - 70% EtOH 1min - 90% EtOH 1min - Twice: 100% EtOH 1min
1st embedding step: 1:1 (v/v) of 100% EtOH: LX112 resin. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood. Biowave settings: MW 250 W, 3 min VAC on
2st embedding step: 100% LX112. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood. Biowave settings: MW 250 W, 3 min VAC on
3rd embedding step: 100% LX112. Perform the liquid exchange in a fume hood. Biowave settings: MW 250 W, 3 min VAC on
Place beem capsules filled with 100% LX112 upside down on top of the neurons in a fume hood.
Polymerization at 60°C 48 h - Samples are polymerized immediately, no need to wait for 2 h
Trim 1 mm x 1 mm pyramid, and thin section (80-90 nm) samples using an ultramicrotome (such as Leica Biosystems, UC6FCS).
For HRP-analysis, TEM images are acquired randomly when HRP signal is observed.

The quantification of the number of HRP-containing synaptic vesicles and endosomes is quantified with Adobe Photoshop Count Tool, and the size (sectional area, µm²) of the HRP-stained endosomes is quantified manually using ImageJ/Fiji ( https://imagej.nih.gov/ij/ )
Wash away all free HRP from the plates using prewarmed (37°C) low K⁺ buffer.

High K+ buffer consists of 0.5 mM MgCl₂ (Chem-Supply, MA029), 2.2 mM CaCl₂ (Sigma-Aldrich, C5080), 56 mM KCl (Ajax Finechem, 1206119), 95 mM NaCl(Amresco, X190), 5.6 mM D-glucose (Amresco, 0188), 0.5 mM ascorbic acid (Sigma-Aldrich, A5960), 0.1% BSA (Sigma-Aldrich,A7638), 15 mM HEPES (Gibco, 15630-080), pH 7.4, 290–310 mOsm.

For high potassium neuronal depolarisation, stimulate neurons in prewarmed (37°C) high K⁺ buffer supplemented with 10 mg/ ml horse radish peroxidase (HRP) and incubate cell incubator at for 5 minutes at 37°C with 5% CO₂. For electric field stimulation assays, add 1 mg/ml HRP diluted in prewarmed (37°C) low K + buffer for the duration of high frequency stimulation (300 APs at 50 Hz for 6 s).

Low K⁺ buffer consists of 0.5 mM MgCl₂ (Chem-Supply, MA029), 2.2 mM CaCl₂ (Sigma-Aldrich,C5080), 5.6 mM KCl (Ajax Finechem, 1206119), 145 mM NaCl (Amresco, X190),5.6 mM D-glucose (Amresco, 0188), 0.5 mM ascorbic acid (Sigma-Aldrich, A5960),0.1% BSA (Sigma-Aldrich, A7638), 15 mM HEPES (Gibco, 15630-080), pH 7.4290–310 mOsm
